title: Unicode Normalization, Case Folding, and Original Evidence · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for unicode normalization, case folding, and original evidence and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, filesystem, terminal, packaging, operating-system, load, security, and release transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for versioned reversible Unicode policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, an…
objectives: Choose NFC, NFD, NFKC, NFKD, or no normalization from the comparison goal and version the decision.
Use casefold for declared caseless matching and test expansions and language-sensitive limitations rather than assuming lower is equivalent.
Retain original spans beside normalized comparison keys so reports remain inspectable and reversible.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Unicode Version Evidence
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Bookbot team handles case 8575 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to version normalization and case policy while preserving inspectable original text; test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Record Unicode database and policy versions and rerun a changed fixture when either changes.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Unicode Version Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Unicode Version Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Bookbot team handles case 5446 during a multilingual community archive intake. Build deterministic Python evidence to version normalization and case policy while preserving inspectable original text; test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Record Unicode database and policy versions and rerun a changed fixture when either changes.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Unicode Version Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving Unicode Version Evidence through the concrete versioned reversible Unicode policy case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Unicode Version Evidence, keep Unicode Version Evidence connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record Unicode database and policy versions and rerun a changed fixture when either changes.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Unicode Version Evidence, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Unicode Version Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Unicode Version Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A quiz Bookbot team handles case 5446 during a multilingual community archive intake. Build deterministic Python evidence to version normalization and case policy while preserving inspectable original text; test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Record Unicode database and policy versions and rerun a changed fixture when either changes.

For Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Unicode Version Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Unicode behavior is timeless and cannot alter a report between Python releases.
